meaning: Literally the water monitor lizard. The ultimate Thai swear word. Historically, these lizards ate villagers' chickens, making them universally hated.
cultural_context: Considered so legally and culturally offensive that the government tried to rename the animal to 'Tua Ngen Tua Thong' (silver and gold creature) to sanitize it. Used as an exclamation (Hia!) or an insult.
example_usage: เหี้ยเอ๊ย! (Hia oey! - Oh fuck/shit!)

word: ไอ้เหี้ย (ai hia)
english_equivalent: fucking bastard / son of a bitch
meaning: The male-directed version of 'hia'. Used to aggressively insult a man.
cultural_context: One of the quickest ways to start a fight with a Thai man. Deeply disrespectful and hostile.
example_usage: มึงมันไอ้เหี้ย (Mueng man ai hia - You are a fucking bastard.)

meaning: The female-directed version of 'hia'. Used to aggressively insult a woman.
cultural_context: An extremely vulgar and degrading insult used against a woman.
example_usage: อีเหี้ยเอ๊ย (Ee hia oey - You fucking bitch.)

meaning: The water buffalo. Used universally to call someone stupid, slow, or ignorant.
cultural_context: Because buffalos are beasts of burden controlled by farmers, calling someone a buffalo means they are a mindless follower or an absolute idiot.
example_usage: โง่เหมือนควาย (Ngo muean khwai - Stupid like a buffalo.)

meaning: Directed at a man to call him extremely stupid.
cultural_context: Very common in arguments driving in Bangkok traffic.
example_usage: ขับรถประสาอะไรไอ้ควาย (Khap rot prasa arai ai khwai - How the fuck are you driving, you idiot?)

meaning: Directed at a woman to call her extremely stupid.
cultural_context: A harsh attack on a woman's intellectual capacity.
example_usage: อีควาย (Ee khwai - You dumb bitch.)

meaning: A vulgar abbreviation of 'sat-dela-chan' (bestial animal).
cultural_context: Often placed at the end of sentences as a crude punctuation mark amongst male friends (e.g., 'Arai wa sat?' = What the fuck, man?).
example_usage: เหงาสัส (Ngao sat - Fucking lonely.)

meaning: Calling a male a beast or lower animal.
cultural_context: Highly aggressive when used in anger against a stranger.
example_usage: ไอ้สัส เอ๊ย (Ai sat oey - You fucking bastard.)

meaning: Male genitalia. Extremely vulgar word for penis.
cultural_context: Used to insult someone or as a general exclamation of frustration. Sometimes abbreviated in texting as 'ค'.
example_usage: เอาควยไปกิน (Ao khuai pai kin - Go eat a dick.)

meaning: Calling a man a dick.
cultural_context: Identical in usage to calling someone a 'dickhead' in English.
example_usage: ไอ้ควยนี่ (Ai khuai ni - This dickhead.)

meaning: Female genitalia. Very rude and crude.
cultural_context: Often combined with 'naa' (face) to make powerful insults.
example_usage: แหกหี (Haek hii - Spread the pussy [vulgar insult]).

meaning: Someone whose face is a vagina.
cultural_context: One of the most degrading physical insults available in the Thai language.
example_usage: ไอ้หน้าหี (Ai naa hii - You cunt-face.)

meaning: The highly vulgar verb for sexual intercourse.
cultural_context: Used for the physical act, but heavily used as a prefix for insults (e.g. Yet-mae = motherfucker).
example_usage: ไปเย็ดกัน (Pai yet kan - Let's go fuck.)

meaning: Fucking one's mother.
cultural_context: A direct and deadly insult. Bringing the mother into a Thai insult elevates it to maximum hostility.
example_usage: ไอ้เย็ดแม่ (Ai yet mae - You motherfucker.)

word: เย็ดแม่ง (yet maeng)
english_equivalent: fuck it / gosh darn it / motherfucker
meaning: A slightly modified version used more as an exclamation when things go wrong.
cultural_context: Used exactly like shouting 'Fuck!' when dropping a tool or failing a test.
example_usage: เย็ดแม่งเอ๊ย (Yet maeng oey - Fuck it all to hell.)

word: เย็ดเป็ด (yet ped)
meaning: An exclamation of surprise or disbelief.
cultural_context: A rhyming, slightly comedic swear phrase. Less offensive than 'yet mae', used more like 'holy crap'.
example_usage: เย็ดเป็ด! ใหญ่มาก (Yet ped! Yai mak - Fuck a duck! That's huge.)

meaning: A shortened clipping of 'mae-man' (its mother). Used strictly as a vulgar intensifier.
cultural_context: Very common in everyday crude speech. 'Mae-ng rorn' = Fucking hot.
example_usage: แม่งเอ๊ย (Maeng oey - Goddammit.)

meaning: Used when something is completely ruined, or used as a strong intensifier for adjectives.
cultural_context: Extremely common. 'Pak waan chip-hai' means 'fucking sweet mouth/smooth talker'.
example_usage: ซวยชิบหาย (Suay chip hai - Fucking unlucky.)

meaning: A classic, traditional Thai insult for a promiscuous or deceitful woman.
cultural_context: Despite sounding beautiful in English, 'golden flower' is one of the most vicious things you can call a Thai woman. Highly stigmatized.
example_usage: อีดอกทอง (Ee dok thong - You golden-flower/slut.)

meaning: A shorthand for 'ee dok thong'.
cultural_context: Very commonly used as slang among gay men (kathoey) to address each other ('Hey bitch!'), but highly offensive if used maliciously against a woman.
example_usage: อีดอกนี่ (Ee dok ni - This bitch.)

meaning: A rhino. Slang for a woman who flirts excessively or acts promiscuously.
cultural_context: Rhinos allegedly breed aggressively. Calling a girl 'raed' means she is desperately chasing men.
example_usage: อีแรด (Ee raed - You slut/rhino.)

meaning: The word for Indian curry, but in street slang, it exclusively means a cheap prostitute.
cultural_context: A direct, degrading slur for a sex worker or a woman acting like one.
example_usage: อีกะหรี่ (Ee karee - You whore.)

meaning: To tell a brazen, malicious lie, or someone who lies constantly.
cultural_context: Mostly directed at women who fabricate drama or lie to look good. A very sharp, biting accusation.
example_usage: อีตอแหล (Ee tor lae - You lying bitch.)

meaning: The highly informal, historically ancient pronoun for 'I'.
cultural_context: Not explicitly a swear word, but using 'Gu' with anyone other than your best friends is considered immediately aggressive and rude. The bedrock of all Thai swearing sentences.
example_usage: กูไม่แคร์ (Gu mai care - I don't fucking care.)

meaning: The highly informal, historically ancient pronoun for 'You'. Paired with 'Gu'.
cultural_context: Pointing at a stranger and calling them 'Mueng' is a challenge to fight.
example_usage: มึงจะเอาอะไร (Mueng ja ao arai - What the fuck do you want?)

word: พ่อมึงตาย (por mueng tai)
english_equivalent: fuck your dad / go to hell
meaning: Declaring that the target's father has died.
cultural_context: A severe provocation. Used as an insult or a reaction to a terrible joke.
example_usage: พ่อมึงตายเหรอ (Por mueng tai roer - Is your dad dead / What the fuck is wrong with you?)

word: แม่มึงตาย (mae mueng tai)
meaning: Declaring that the target's mother has died.
cultural_context: Just as offensive as 'por mueng tai'. Attacks the sanctity of the Thai family structure.
example_usage: แม่มึงตาย (Mae mueng tai - Fuck your mom.)

meaning: The heel of the foot. In Thai culture, feet are the lowest and dirtiest part of the body.
cultural_context: Pointing feet at someone is rude; calling them the heel of a foot is incredibly degrading.
example_usage: ไอ้ส้นตีน (Ai son teen - You bottom-feeding piece of shit.)

meaning: Someone whose face resembles a foot.
cultural_context: Combining the highest, most sacred part of the body (head/face) with the lowest (foot) is a severe cultural insult.
example_usage: ไอ้หน้าตีน (Ai naa teen - You foot-face.)

word: กวนส้นตีน (kuan son teen)
literal_translation: stirring the heel of the foot
english_equivalent: asking for a kick / being an annoying prick
meaning: Acting in a highly provocative or annoying manner.
cultural_context: Literally means you are stirring up my foot so much that it wants to kick you.
example_usage: อย่ามากวนส้นตีน (Ya ma kuan son teen - Don't be a provocative prick.)

meaning: A direct command to perform fellatio.
cultural_context: Highly vulgar and aggressive way to dismiss someone.
example_usage: ไปอมควย (Pai om khuai - Go suck a dick.)

meaning: Feces. Used as a prefix for many negative adjectives.
cultural_context: For example, 'khee-niao' (sticky shit = stingy), 'khee-kiat' (lazy shit = lazy). Or by itself for feces.
example_usage: ปวดขี้ (Puat khee - I need to shit.)

word: ไอ้ขี้หมา (ai khee maa)
meaning: A worthless, disgusting person.
cultural_context: Dog feces on the street is common and despised.
example_usage: มึงมันไอ้ขี้หมา (Mueng man ai khee maa - You are a piece of dog shit.)

meaning: A man who lives off a woman's income, usually a pimp.
cultural_context: Male horseshoe crabs attach themselves to the females and ride them. Hence, a pimp who leeches off prostitutes.
example_usage: ไอ้แมงดา (Ai maeng da - You leech/pimp.)

meaning: A dumb or crazy man.
cultural_context: A very mild, common insult. Can be playful among friends or angry in traffic.
example_usage: ไอ้บ้าเอ๊ย (Ai baa oey - You crazy fool.)

meaning: A dumb or crazy woman.
cultural_context: Often yelled in soap operas (Lakorn) before a slap.
example_usage: อีบ้า (Ee baa - You crazy woman.)

meaning: A dog. Dogs are considered lowly street animals in traditional Thai culture.
cultural_context: Used as an insult to say someone is low-class or behaving badly.
example_usage: สันดานหมา (Sandan maa - Dog nature / acting like a stray dog.)

meaning: Son of a bitch.
cultural_context: Demeaning someone's lineage.
example_usage: ไอ้ลูกหมา (Ai luk maa - You son of a bitch.)

word: อีหอกแหก (ee hok haek)
meaning: A woman who is incompetent, disastrous, or sexually loose.
cultural_context: An older slang term that implies something completely broken and useless.
example_usage: อีหอกแหกนี่ (Ee hok haek ni - This broken-spear bitch.)

word: หน้าด้าน (naa daan)
meaning: Someone who has no shame or morals.
cultural_context: In a 'face' saving culture, calling someone thick-faced means they are indifferent to absolute disgrace.
example_usage: อีหน้าด้าน (Ee naa daan - You shameless bitch.)

word: หน้าตัวเมีย (naa tua mia)
meaning: A deeply degrading insult strictly for men who hit women or act in an unchivalrous, cowardly manner.
cultural_context: Implies the man has stripped away all his masculinity and honor.
example_usage: ไอ้หน้าตัวเมีย (Ai naa tua mia - You unmanly coward.)

meaning: Something or someone that is unforgivably evil or twisted in character.
cultural_context: A very serious moral condemnation.
example_usage: คนระยำ (Khon rayam - A wicked/despicable person.)

word: ชาติชั่ว (chat chua)
meaning: Someone whose very incarnation (chat) in this life is evil (chua).
cultural_context: A karmic insult. You are saying they were born entirely rotten.
example_usage: ไอ้ชาติชั่ว (Ai chat chua - You born-evil bastard.)

meaning: Something deeply taboo, cursed, or disgustingly vile.
cultural_context: Used to describe incest, extreme perversion, or a truly terrible person.
example_usage: ไอ้จัญไร (Ai janrai - You vile scum.)

word: เจ๊าะแจ๊ะ (jo jae)
meaning: Someone who talks too much nonsense or flirts annoyingly.
cultural_context: Very mild. Used to tell someone to shut up and stop bothering you.
example_usage: อย่ามาเจ๊าะแจ๊ะ (Ya ma jo jae - Don't come flirting/bothering.)

word: อีร้อยควย (ee roi khuai)
meaning: A woman who has slept with a hundred men.
cultural_context: Extremely graphic and vulgar street insult.
example_usage: อีร้อยควย (Ee roi khuai - You hundred-dick slut.)

meaning: Hoi means clam/shellfish, which is standard slang for vagina.
cultural_context: A slightly more creative, aquatic way to call a woman a cunt.
example_usage: อีหอยเอ๊ย (Ee hoi oey - You fucking clam/cunt.)

english_equivalent: nosy / meddling / mind your own business
meaning: To push your nose into someone else's business.
cultural_context: Highly aggressive way to tell someone to mind their own fucking business.
example_usage: อย่าเสือก (Ya sueak - Don't meddle / Mind your own fucking business.)

meaning: Clitoris.
cultural_context: Often used in colorful insults similar to 'hii'.
example_usage: ใหญ่เท่าแตดมด (Yai thao taet mot - Big as an ant's clit / i.e., tiny.)

meaning: The standard Thai word for a transgender woman or effeminate gay man.
cultural_context: While it is the standard vocabulary word, when used by straight men as a derogatory label, it acts as a slur.
example_usage: ไอ้กะเทย (Ai kathoey - You tranny.)

word: ลูกกะหรี่ (luk karee)
meaning: Son or daughter of a prostitute.
cultural_context: Similar to luk maa (son of a dog), but explicitly attacks the mother's sexual morality.
example_usage: ไอ้ลูกกะหรี่ (Ai luk karee - You son of a whore.)

meaning: Bad karma manifesting in the present. Used when something unfortunate happens.
cultural_context: A Buddhist swearing concept. 'Ai wen' (You bad karma) means 'You curse/burden'.
example_usage: ไอ้เวรเอ๊ย (Ai wen oey - You damn burden.)

meaning: In Buddhist mythology, a pret is a cursed, impossibly tall ghost with a needle-thin mouth, suffering eternal hunger.
cultural_context: Used to insult someone who is abnormally tall and skinny, or someone who is constantly greedy and seeking handouts.
example_usage: ไอ้เปรต (Ai pret - You hungry ghost.)

word: ไปตายซะ (pai tai sa)
meaning: Directly telling someone to go and die.
cultural_context: Standard dramatic soap-opera phrase for ending an argument.
example_usage: ไปตายซะไป (Pai tai sa pai - Go drop dead.)

meaning: Someone who is incredibly rude, uneducated, and vulgar.
cultural_context: A class-based insult criticizing someone's lack of manners and upbringing.
example_usage: ไอ้ถ่อย (Ai thoi - You low-class scum.)

word: มารหัวขน (man hua khon)
literal_translation: demon with hair on its head
meaning: A harsh, archaic insult for an unwanted child or infant.
cultural_context: Implies the child is a demon that should not have been born. Deeply cruel.
example_usage: ไอ้มารหัวขน (Ai man hua khon - You hairy demon child.)

word: ปัญญาอ่อน (panya on)
meaning: The medical term for mental retardation, used as a heavy insult for stupidity.
cultural_context: Exactly equivalent to calling an adult purely foolish or 'retarded'.
example_usage: มึงปัญญาอ่อนเหรอ (Mueng panya on roer - Are you fucking retarded?)

meaning: Garbage.
cultural_context: Used metaphorically to call someone a piece of human trash.
example_usage: ไอ้ขยะสังคม (Ai khaya sangkhom - You social trash.)

meaning: Someone who was incarnated into this life strictly to be an idiot.
cultural_context: Combines karma with stupidity.
example_usage: ไอ้ชาติโง่ (Ai chat ngo - You born-idiot.)

meaning: Something terrible, wretched, or an exclamation of failure.
cultural_context: Older vocabulary. Often translated as 'damn it' in Thai subtitles for foreign movies.
example_usage: ชีวิตบัดซบ (Chiwit bat sob - Wretched life.)

word: เวรตะไล (wen ta lai)
meaning: An amplified version of 'wen' (karma). A massive burden or a terrible person.
cultural_context: A classic, slightly poetic sounding exclamation of severe annoyance.
example_usage: ไอ้เวรตะไล (Ai wen ta lai - You blazing bastard.)

meaning: Pirate. Used historically to call someone a bandit or scoundrel.
cultural_context: A safe-for-TV villain insult.
example_usage: ไอ้สลัด (Ai salat - You scoundrel.)

word: ไปไกลๆตีนกู (pai glai glai teen gu)
literal_translation: go far far away from my foot
english_equivalent: get the fuck out of my sight / or I'll kick you
meaning: Telling someone to back off immediately before they get kicked.
cultural_context: Very aggressive. 'Gu' (My) + 'Teen' (Foot) is heavily disrespectful.
example_usage: ไปไกลๆตีนกูเลย (Pai glai glai teen gu loey - Get the fuck away from my feet right now.)

meaning: An effeminate gay man. Derived from the US movie 'Tootsie'.
cultural_context: Often used casually, but acts as a homophobic slur when used to demean a straight man or mock a gay man.
example_usage: ทำตัวเป็นตุ๊ด (Tham tua pen tut - Acting like a sissy/fag.)

meaning: A vagina that smells terrible.
cultural_context: A highly graphic, childish but deeply insulting thing to say to a woman.
example_usage: อีหีเหม็น (Ee hii men - You stinking cunt.)

word: แม่งเอ๊ย (maeng oey)
meaning: The ultimate exclamation of frustration. Milder than 'Yet mae' but still vulgar.
cultural_context: Used exactly like slipping up and saying 'dammit' in English.
example_usage: แม่งเอ๊ย ลืมกุญแจ (Maeng oey, luem kun-jae - Goddammit, I forgot my keys.)

meaning: Thai phonetic spelling of the English word 'fuck'.
cultural_context: Because it's foreign, Thais use it constantly on TV and in songs to sound rebellious without triggering censorship on native Thai curses.
example_usage: ฟักยู (Fak yu - Fuck you.)
